methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $5.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Helion is a Series F frontier-energy company founded by David Kirtley and Chris Pihl, having raised $425M+ to date at a current post-money valuation of $5.4B. The relevant peer set comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) across the Climate & Energy sector, including TAE Technologies, Form Energy, Watershed, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Redwood Materials.

Two valuation methods apply to Helion's current stage. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement, implies a forward valuation of $7.0B from the $5.4B base. Peer-median triangulation across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—TAE Technologies ($6.0B Series G), Form Energy ($3.5B Series G), and Watershed ($1.8B Series G)—yields a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range of $1.8B to $6.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $3.0B (low) to $8.1B (high), with a median of $5.3B. This bracketing places Helion's current $5.4B valuation near the midpoint, reflecting reasonable consistency with stage-typical progression but meaningful variance in peer comparables. Key uncertainties stem from the limited peer set size (n=3 for median analysis) and stage heterogeneity within the climate-tech universe, where valuation dispersion remains wide.
